evals.prompt: Check whether docs/policy.md is a conformant MIF document at Level 1.
evals.expected_output: Runs mif-validate against the file at --level 1 and reports the deterministic VALID/INVALID verdict.
expectations: Invokes scripts/mif-validate.mjs with the file and --level 1
Reports schema conformance, the level floor, and round-trip result
Treats a non-zero exit as a hard failure (fail-closed), not a warning
Does not assert validity by inspection without running the gate

evals.prompt: Convert docs/policy.md to its JSON-LD form so a machine consumer can read it.
evals.expected_output: Runs mif-convert emit-jsonld to produce the canonical JSON-LD projection.
expectations: Invokes scripts/mif-convert.mjs emit-jsonld on the markdown file
Explains the JSON-LD is the schema-checked canonical form
Notes the projection is lossless and reversible via emit-markdown

evals.prompt: I have a MIF JSON-LD object from an API. Give me the human-readable markdown version.
evals.expected_output: Schema-checks the JSON-LD, then runs emit-markdown to project it to markdown.
expectations: Invokes scripts/mif-convert.mjs emit-markdown on the JSON file
Validates the JSON-LD against the canonical schema before projecting
Produces markdown with YAML frontmatter plus body

evals.prompt: Prove that converting this document to JSON-LD and back loses no information.
evals.expected_output: Runs the roundtrip oracle and reports lossless or fails closed.
expectations: Invokes scripts/mif-convert.mjs roundtrip (or mif-validate which includes the round-trip)
Reports the markdown<->JSON-LD round-trip as lossless on success
Fails non-zero if information is lost

evals.prompt: The validator says the schema isn't hydrated. What do I do?
evals.expected_output: Runs npm run hydrate-schema to refresh the canonical schema cache from mif-spec.dev.
expectations: Explains the bundled schema is a refreshable cache, not the authority
Notes offline fallback to the last hydrated version with a staleness warning
